title: rubus serpens var. brevipes
intro: Rubus serpens var. brevipes, also known as creeping raspberry or short-stemmed dewberry, is a variety of the Rubus serpens plant, a type of bramble native to eastern North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its berries and roots. The health benefits of Rubus serpens var. brevipes include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
